positiveClaims: The existing release claim user-facing boundary gate is product-bound as bounded internal Review Bridge 12G boundary admission.
Accepted 12G requires accepted 12F packet emit provenance with accepted packet emit type, status, code, and reason.
Accepted 12G requires packet emit binding, packet, and strict report fields to agree on mode, claimId, dossierId, matrixId, and releaseClass.
Accepted 12G requires raw packet emit binding fields for mode, claimId, dossierId, matrixId, releaseClass, packetId, and attestationId; these fields are not derived from packet or report fallbacks.
Accepted 12G requires packet/report/summary packetHash linkage to match a recomputed release claim packet hash.
Accepted 12G requires packetId and attestationId linkage to match across packet emit binding, packet, and strict report.
Accepted 12G binds INTERNAL and USER_FACING claim surfaces only as boundary surfaces.
RELEASE_MODE USER_FACING boundary admission is accepted only when the accepted 12F packet class is USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY.
Accepted 12G emits a deterministic non-mutating boundary result with fixed top-level, binding, and summary keys.
The 12G boundary runtime now strips inherited prototype fields before boundary input and packetEmitResult acceptance checks.
This contour is user-facing boundary binding truth only; it is not release readiness, release execution, release publication, import, export, apply, storage truth, or project truth.
nonClaims: No release execution completion is claimed.
No release publication completion is claimed.
No release-wide publication authority is claimed.
No Google Docs support is claimed.
No DOCX support expansion is claimed.
No review import completion is claimed.
No manuscript write is performed by release claim user-facing boundary binding.
No project truth write is performed by release claim user-facing boundary binding.
No receipt or recovery evidence is created by release claim user-facing boundary binding.
No DOCX import safe-create behavior is changed.
No DOCX export behavior is changed.
No import/export MVP closeout is widened.
No PDF, EPUB, HTML, Mindmap, cloud sync, cross-platform readiness, packaged readiness, or Y9 admission is claimed.
layerDecisions: User-facing boundary binding is boundary admission only.
User-facing boundary binding does not become project truth by itself.
User-facing boundary binding does not write manuscript truth or project truth.
User-facing boundary binding does not execute release commands.
User-facing boundary binding does not publish anything.
User-facing boundary binding does not admit release execution or release publication.
USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY remains an internal packet and boundary class only, not a release readiness state.
USER_FACING is a boundary surface in this contour, not a user-facing UI state.
This contour is not a release execution contour.
This contour is not a release publication contour.
This contour is not content import and does not create scenes.
This contour is not Review import automation and does not activate a Review session.
This contour is not an apply plan and does not authorize mutation.
This contour is not safe write and does not create receipt or recovery evidence.
This contour is not export and does not alter DOCX output.
Review Bridge may reference accepted 12G output as bounded internal chain evidence, but accepted 12G output does not become project truth.

machineChecks.command: node --test release claim user-facing boundary kernel contracts
node --test release claim user-facing boundary binding contracts
node --test release claim user-facing boundary binding plus adjacent 12B through 12G contracts
node --test release claim full contract pack

agentAudits.finding: Confirmed inherited-prototype input is stripped by 12G gate normalization and identified a coherent synthetic accepted packet bypass; resolved by requiring packet/report/summary hash linkage plus packetId and attestationId linkage checks.

Confirmed 12G had kernel tests but no dedicated status or governance binding; required scope, nonclaims, layer decisions, proof points, docs sync, and forbidden layer-mixing phrases were added for bounded boundary admission only.

Found a false green where an otherwise coherent accepted packet emit result with empty raw binding could pass because binding values were derived from packet/report fallback fields. Resolved by requiring raw binding fields for mode, claimId, dossierId, matrixId, releaseClass, packetId, and attestationId, adding empty-binding and missing-binding-id negative contracts, and making docs require that USER_FACING is not a UI state.

nextStep: finish delivery chain; after delivery, select the next owner-approved boundary explicitly; release execution, release publication, and post-MVP import/export expansion do not open by implication
